item.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
item.description:
The critical, three-stage attack is now patched, but it's part of a new group of AI prompt-injection issues that use hidden URLs and other variables.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: HTTP/2 Bomb Attacks Put Telcos, Healthcare Orgs at Risk
item.description:
The denial-of-service (DoS) exploit takes advantage of two features in HTTP/2 that were designed to save Internet bandwith, not power massive amplification attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'Lorem Ipsum' Malware Pivots to ClickFix Delivery
item.description:
New analysis shows the campaign, which uses compromised WordPress sites, may be linked to the ransomware and data extortion group Vice Society.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
item.description:
An open letter signed by dozens of security experts asked the government to reverse export restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
item.description:
Attackers are actively targeting various sectors across nearly 200 countries and already have compiled a list of working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised devices.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
item.description:
And one of those basics is focusing on sectors where a ransomware disruption creates immediate pressure to pay up, like with healthcare.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: EU Gets a Head Start in Developing 6G Network Security
item.description:
"Shield-6G" will combine AI threat detection, digital twins, honeypots, and more, to help carriers protect 6G networks against the threats of tomorrow.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Get Out of Security Debt by Tackling the Exposure Problem
item.description:
Teams digging out of security debt need to answer only two simple questions: Which vulnerabilities in our systems are exposed, and how long should they stay that way?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise
item.description:
Klue's Battlecards is now the third integrated application that has been compromised to steal customers' Salesforce data, and victims include Huntress, the cybersecurity vendor.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Operation Escaneo Signals Shift in LatAm Threat Landscape
item.description:
The threat group's curious business model may combine opportunistic monetization alongside intel collection, without much coordination between the two.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk
item.description:
A leaked GitHub token underscores what most organizations get wrong: Treating secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity problem.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Stressors, AI Forcing Changes to Cybersecurity Teams
item.description:
As threats proliferate and AI complicates cybersecurity, CISOs say the job is getting harder, but more companies still want cybersecurity expertise, if even on a part-time basis.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor
item.description:
CryptoBandits uses a local SOCKS5 proxy for traffic routing, blending data theft with remote code execution. The post CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Texas Parks & Wildlife Data Breach Affects 3 Million Individuals
item.description:
Hackers stole personal information after breaching the systems of a third-party license vendor serving TPWD. The post Texas Parks & Wildlife Data Breach Affects 3 Million Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: More Cybersecurity Firms Disclose Impact From Klue Hack
item.description:
HackerOne, Huntress, Jamf, OneTrust, Recorded Future, Snyk, and Tanium are among the affected Klue customers. The post More Cybersecurity Firms Disclose Impact From Klue Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones
item.description:
The vulnerability exploited by the Usbliter8 exploit cannot be patched and a PoC exploit has been released by researchers. The post New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
item.description:
Groups like ShinyHunters are demonstrating that attackers do not necessarily need malware or zero-day exploits to cause massive damage. The post What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
A malicious dependency the attackers added to over 140 Mastra packages fetches a payload targeting cryptocurrency extensions. The post North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data
item.description:
Vulnerable WordPress plugin iterations leak API keys, secrets, tokens, server information, and other data. The post Attackers Exploit Gravity SMTP Plugin Flaw to Harvest Valuable WordPress Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
item.description:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: HIPAA Compliance for Medical Spas
item.description:
Medical spas that collect health histories, administer injectable treatments, perform laser procedures, bill electronically for covered health care services, or […] The post HIPAA Compliance for Medical Spas appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: HIPAA Training for Medical Spas
item.description:
Medical spas that qualify as HIPAA-Covered Entities should provide all members of their workforce with HIPAA training that covers foundational […] The post HIPAA Training for Medical Spas appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Heart Monitoring Device Manufacturer Discloses Cyberattack; Data Breach
item.description:
iRhythm Holdings Inc., a publicly traded heart monitoring device manufacturer, has notified the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) about […] The post Heart Monitoring Device Manufacturer Discloses Cyberattack; Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: HIPAA Violation Cases: Types & Consequences
item.description:
HIPAA violation cases are compliance investigations that result from a data breach being reported to the Department of Health and […] The post HIPAA Violation Cases: Types & Consequences appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: HIPAA Violation Fines
item.description:
HIPAA violation fines can be issued by the Department of Health and Human Services’ Office for Civil Rights (OCR) and […] The post HIPAA Violation Fines appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Spencer Gifts Pays $450,000 Penalty to Resolve HIPAA Failures
item.description:
The national retail company Spencer Gifts LLC has agreed to a $450,000 settlement to resolve alleged violations of the HIPAA […] The post Spencer Gifts Pays $450,000 Penalty to Resolve HIPAA Failures appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Multi-million-dollar Settlement Agreed to Resolve MCNA Dental Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
A settlement has been agreed to resolve class action data breach litigation against Managed Care of North America (MCNA), Inc., […] The post Multi-million-dollar Settlement Agreed to Resolve MCNA Dental Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: ShinyHunters Data Extortion Group Threatens to Leak 8.8 TB of Stolen One Medical Data
item.description:
One Medical, the Amazon-owned primary care provider, has recently announced a cybersecurity incident in which an unauthorized third party gained […] The post ShinyHunters Data Extortion Group Threatens to Leak 8.8 TB of Stolen One Medical Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Nintendo confirms data stolen in WebMD subsidiary cyberattack
item.description:
Nintendo of America has confirmed to BleepingComputer that threat actors stole survey data from the third-party TinyPulse service used internally, but its systems were not compromised. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Gentlemen ransomware uses multiple EDR killers to disable defenses
item.description:
The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers to help affiliates evade detection in attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) urged Fortinet customers to secure their devices after nearly 74,000 firewall and VPN credentials were exposed in a data leak dubbed "FortiBleed." [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
item.description:
Modern phishing attacks, including Device Code phishing, can undermine MFA protections and grant attackers access to corporate accounts without stealing passwords. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help security teams detect compromised accounts faster and automate response workflows. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Texas govt data breach exposes over 3 million driver’s licenses
item.description:
The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department (TPWD) disclosed a data breach at its license system vendor that exposed personal information for more than three million individuals. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin
item.description:
Threat actors are exploiting an unauthenticated information disclosure vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Gravity SMTP, active on 100,000 sites. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
item.description:
Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, as the new "Icarus" extortion group publicly claims the attack. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Microsoft links Mastra AI supply chain attack to North Korean hackers
item.description:
Microsoft has attributed a recent Mastra AI supply chain attack that compromised more than 140 npm packages to the North Korean hacking group Sapphire Sleet, also known as BlueNoroff. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New Prinz Eugen ransomware prioritizes recent files for encryption
item.description:
A new ransomware operation named 'Prinz Eugen' prioritizes recently modified files for encryption and leaves no ransom note on the system. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: HHS’ Oﬃce for Civil Rights Settles Ransomware Investigation with Spencer Gifts Health Plan for $450k, Corrective Action Plan
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), Office for Civil Rights (OCR) today announced a settlement with Spencer Gifts LLC Flexible Beneﬁts and Welfare Beneﬁt Plans (the Plan), the employer-sponsored group health plan of Spencer Gifts LLC, a national retail company, over potential violations of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Texas government data breach allowed hackers to steal 3 million driver’s licenses and passports
item.description:
Zack Whittaker reports: A data breach at a Texas state government department allowed hackers to take the driver’s license information and passport numbers of more than 3 million people, according to the state’s attorney general. The incident is one of the largest data breaches to affect the state this year. In a data breach notice on the Texas... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Amazon-Owned One Medical Faces Alleged 8.8TB Data Breach
item.description:
Emily Hill reports: One Medical, the primary care provider acquired by Amazon in 2023, is facing questions after the cybercriminal group ShinyHunters claimed it stole 8.8 terabytes of company data and threatened to publish the information unless negotiations begin by June 22. The allegation remains unverified, and the group has not released any sample data to support... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Blue Fish Pediatrics notifies 41,485 Texans about data breach last year
item.description:
Ahmed Humble reports that 41,485 Texans may have had personal and protected health information exposed in a data breach involving a Houston-based Blue Fish Pediatrics. The breach reportedly occurred between July 11 and July 17, 2025, but patients are only first being notified now. The types of information included: Full names Dates of birth Social... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Canadian hacker pleads guilty to charges for cyberattack on Texas Republican website
item.description:
Alexandra Posadzki reports: Canadian hacker Aubrey Cottle has pleaded guilty to three charges stemming from a cyberattack linked to notorious hacktivist group Anonymous on the Texas Republican Party. Mr. Cottle, who appeared in court in Newmarket, Ont., on Thursday, pleaded guilty to fraudulently obtaining a computer service, namely the systems of web-hosting company Epik, causing mischief... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Bombay High Court Blocks FulcrumSec Data Leak (3)
item.description:
Another day, another injunction. When DataBreaches read the news headline, our first thought was that this was an injunction sought by Global Schools Group. Our first impression was correct, but it took a reminder from FulcrumSec to realize that it was GSG-connected. Ananya Iyer reports: The Bombay High Court issued an interim order restraining the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Global Schools Group Obtained Two Court Injunctions That Didn’t Seem to Change Much—and Might Backfire (1)
item.description:
Following a major data security incident involving sensitive student and parent information, Global Schools Group sought court injunctions prohibiting the publication of data acquired by FulcrumSec. They obtained the injunctions, but once again, injunctions do not affect threat actors — or at least, not in the way the plaintiffs hoped. Yesterday, DataBreaches reported that Global... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
item.description:
Lawrence Abrams reports: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers’ Salesforce environments, as the new “Icarus” extortion group publicly claims the attack. The disclosure comes after cybersecurity firms Huntress and ReliaQuest detailed how attackers abused compromised Klue Battlecards integrations to steal Salesforce... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Two Data Breaches Didn’t Sink Novo Nordisk’s Stock. Why Not?
item.description:
June was a challenging month for Novo Nordisk regarding cybersecurity and intellectual property protection. The pharma giant allegedly had some of its data — including intellectual property — stolen by two independent groups of threat actors. Unaware of each other, each group claimed to have acquired a large amount of valuable information. One demanded $25... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Brazil’s Civil Defense suffers a cyberattack on its official alert network
item.description:
This is the kind of cyberattack that can put lives at risk and makes me want to wring some necks if I wasn’t so old and feeble. Demócrata reports: Brazil’s Civil Defense has reported this Saturday that its official alert system has been the target of a cyberattack, an incident that is already being investigated... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: DaikyoNishikawa Report Victim daikyonishikawa.co.jp Threat Actor LockBit Date Discovered Jun 19, 2026 Description DaikyoNishikawa Corporation is an automotive manufacturing company â¦June 19, 2026
item.description:
DaikyoNishikawa Report Victim daikyonishikawa.co.jp Threat Actor LockBit Date Discovered Jun 19, 2026 Description DaikyoNishikawa Corporation is an automotive manufacturing company â¦June 19, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to TheGentlemen.

item.title: Clinical Registry Solutions
